back_translation: I speak the truth in Christ — I am not lying — with my conscience bearing witness together with me in the Holy (royal) Spirit
That I have great sorrow and unceasing pain in my heart-mind
For I myself would wish to be cursed and cut off from Christ for the sake of my brothers — my own kin according to the flesh

Those people are the people of Israel — to whom He (royally) gave the right-of-being-a-son, the (royal) glory, the covenants, the giving of the Law, the (royal) worship-service, and the (royal) promises
Of them are the patriarchs — and from them, Christ (royally) descended according to the flesh — He who (royally) is God over all things, who (royally) is praised forever and ever — Amen
Not as if the (royal) word of God has failed — for not all who descend from Israel are Israel
And not because they are Abraham's offspring are they all children — but 'Through Isaac your offspring will be named'
That is — not the children of the flesh are children of God — but the children of the (royal) promise are (royally) reckoned as offspring
For the word of (royal) promise is this: 'At this appointed time I will return — and Sarah will have a son'
Not only that — but Rebecca conceived by one man — namely Isaac our forefather
For when both had not yet been born — and had not yet done anything good or evil — so that the (royal) purpose of God according to the (royal) choosing might stand
Not from conduct — but from the One who (royally) calls — He (royally) said to Rebecca: 'The older will serve the younger'
Just as it is written: 'Jacob I loved — but Esau I hated'
Therefore, what shall we say? Is God (royally) unrighteous? Absolutely not
For He (royally) speaks to Moses: 'I will have mercy on whom I have mercy — and I will have compassion on whom I have compassion'
Therefore, it does not depend on the one who desires nor the one who runs in a race — but on God who (royally) shows mercy

For the Scripture (royally) speaks to Pharaoh: 'For this very reason I have raised you up — so that I may display My power in you — and so that My (royal) name may be proclaimed throughout all the earth'
Therefore, He (royally) shows mercy to whom He (royally) desires — and (royally) makes hard-hearted whom He (royally) desires
Therefore, you will say to me: 'Then why does He still (royally) find fault? For who can resist His (royal) will?'
O human — truly, who are you to talk back to God? Will the thing that is formed say to the One who formed it: 'Why did you make me like this?'
Or does the potter not have authority over the clay — from the same lump — to make one vessel for honor and another for low-status?
And if God (royally) wills to display (royal) wrath and (royally) make known His power — bearing with great (royal) restraint of mind toward vessels of (royal) wrath that are prepared for destruction
And so that He may (royally) make known the riches of His (royal) glory upon vessels of (royal) mercy — which He (royally) prepared in advance for (royal) glory
Whom He (royally) called — namely us — not only from the Jews but also from the Gentiles
Just as He (royally) speaks in the book of Hosea: 'I will call those who are not my people, my people — and I will call her who is not beloved, beloved'
And: 'It will happen that in the place where it was said to them: "You are not my people" — there they will be called: "sons of the living God"'
And Isaiah cries out concerning Israel: 'Even if the number of the sons of Israel were like the sand of the sea — only the remnant will be saved
For the Lord will (royally) complete and cut short His (royal) word over the earth'
And as Isaiah said in advance: 'Unless the Lord of Hosts had (royally) left us offspring — we would have become like the city of Sodom — and resembled the city of Gomorrah'
Therefore, what shall we say? The Gentiles, who did not pursue righteousness, have obtained righteousness — namely, the righteousness from faith
But Israel, who pursued the Law of righteousness, did not arrive at that Law
Why? Because not from faith — but as if from conduct — they stumbled at the stone of stumbling
Just as it is written: 'Behold — I lay a stone of stumbling and a rock of obstruction in Zion — and the one who believes in Him will not be disappointed at all'
